comment.en: Relates a Property (ObservableProperty or ActuableProperty) to the MetricAssessment that evaluated it (actual level). Inverse of mqto:evaluatesProperty. Counterpart to mqto:readBy in the specified level.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to represent the dateTime at which an activity ended.

usageNote.value: Assume y is an 'activity profile', obtained from some containing activity z by selective abstraction on a quality q of a participant in z. Then, relate y by 'profile quantity datum' to magnitudes of q that are recorded during the execution of y.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x has 'profile quantity datum' y, then x is an 'activity profile', and x 'has participant quality quantified as' y.

example.value: The pressure of a liquid is measured at 10, 11, and 12 bar at different points of time during a transport activity.

comment.undefined: Intuitively, 'profile quantity datum' should only point to quantity data recorded while the activity is taking place. This can not be enforced with OWL restrictions on the relation (it would require a "diamond" pattern). However, with a suitable date or time data property value restriction, an individual activity can constrain related quantity data.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'has spatial extent'.

comment.en: Indicates a moderate-strength causal relationship where one Failure Mode is caused by another with medium probability or influence. Enables ordinal ranking of causal strengths without requiring precise numeric probabilities. Subproperty of causedBy.
Links a MetricAssessment to the FailureOccurrence it detected or triggered. Establishes which failures were raised by threshold violations in metric evaluations. Inverse of raisedBy.
Indicates that a Metric is referred to by a Failure Mode for diagnostic purposes. Establishes which failures use which metrics for detection or characterization. Inverse of refersToMetric.

The timestamp when an activity or time-bound entity concluded. General-purpose temporal property applicable to various execution contexts including MethodExecution, CountermeasureExecution, and time windows.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to represent the language of a Resource (e.g., a Procedure).

comment.en: Associates a Metric with the IshikawaItem (Machine, Manpower, Material, Measurement, or Method) category it monitors or assesses. Establishes which root cause category a metric is used for. Inverse of hasMetric.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'resides in'.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link an Activity (e.g., a Step Execution) to the Agent that confirmed the Activity.

comment.en: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link an Activity (e.g., a Step Execution) to the Agent that confirmed the Activity.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Duration of time to the Temporal Unit which provides the precision of a date-time value or scale of a temporal extent.
In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Step to the Variable that the Step needs to take as input for the Step Execution.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'has arranged part'.
For annotations, see the inverse relation 'profile of quality'.
For annotations, see the inverse relation 'is about'.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to a version of a procedure to the Procedure, for which we want to manage version control.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: x 'has physical quantity' y if and only if x 'has quality' y, and y is a 'physical quantity'.

example.value: A section of pipe has length, a crane has lifting power, a flange has inner diameter.

comment.en: Links a CountermeasureExecution to the FailureOccurrence it addressed (actual level). Inverse of mitigatedByExecution. The specified-level counterpart is mqto:mitigates (domain: Countermeasure, range: FailureMode).
The timestamp when a CountermeasureStepExecution or CountermeasureExecution was aborted. Records the moment the procedure was interrupted before normal completion.

A relative ranking weight assigned to a Failure Mode or Countermeasure. For Failure Modes, indicates severity or priority. For Countermeasures, indicates effectiveness or preference. Provides anchor points for external ranking logic; the ontology does not prescribe the ranking algorithm itself.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to represent the name/title of an entity, including: a Procedure, a Step, a Procedure Execution, a Step Execution, a Step Verification, a FAQ Category, a FAQ Target, a Resource that is referenced by or which a Procedure was extracted from.

usageNote.value: When introducing shortcut properties as subproperties, annotations should be provided to explain the intended quantity type, any quantification type (e.g., measured, nominal, etc.), and unit of measure.
This relation is suitable as the top node in a taxonomy of "shortcut" relations that combine quality and unit of measure into a single, simple data property.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x has 'quality quantity value' y, then x is an 'object', and y is a literal value that quantifies a quality of x.

example.value: Mass in kilograms, pressure in barg.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'has functional part'.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to represent the dateTime at which an InstantaneousEvent occurred.
In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a (Multi)Step of a Procedure to the Error it addresses. Such (Multi)Step represents a Fallback procedure.

comment.en: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a (Multi)Step of a Procedure to the Error it addresses. Such (Multi)Step represents a Fallback procedure.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to represent the motivation for creating a new version of a Procedure, for which we want to manage access control.

comment.en: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to represent the motivation for creating a new version of a Procedure, for which we want to manage access control.

usageNote.value: The 'site' of an object will occupy different 'spatial location's over time if the location and size of the object changes.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has spatial extent' y, then x is a 'physical object', and y is the 'spatial location' determined as the extension in space of x.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of  "spatial extension" of an object is not fully defined.

example.value: The volume occupied by an process skid, the volume occupied by a volume of flammable gas.

comment.undefined: BFO_0000210 is 'occupies spatial region at some time' (from BFO-ISO)

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: x 'has interest' y if and only if x is an 'organization' or 'organism', x 'has potential' y, and y is an 'interest'.

example.value: An intention to start a process, a plan to modify a plant, an approval of a certification.

comment.undefined: Derived from the relation 'lifecycle_stage' of ISO 15926-2:2003, which has the attribute pair 'interest'/'interested'; 'class_of_lifecycle_stage' provides examples of specialisations as, "EXAMPLE Planned, required, expected, and proposed can be represented by instances of [class_of_lifecycle_stage]." Following this, 'interest of' is a superproperty suitable for various intentional relationships, such as planning, approving, or ordering. With IDO, we restrict interest to 'potential' entities, while in ISO 15926-2:2003 the domain and range are both unrestricted.

comment.en: The timestamp when this failure occurrence actually happened in the physical world. Essential for failure frequency analysis, MTBF calculations, and temporal correlation with process events and observations.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Procedure to its Procedure Target(s), that is the object at which the actions of the Procedure are directed. A Procedure Target can be, for instance: a Machine, an Equipment, a Workstation.

comment.en: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Procedure to its Procedure Target(s), that is the object at which the actions of the Procedure are directed. A Procedure Target can be, for instance: a Machine, an Equipment, a Workstation.

definition.value: A relation between an Observation and the entity whose quality was observed, or between an Actuation and the entity whose property was modified, or between an act of Sampling and the entity that was sampled.

example.value: For example, in an Observation of the weight of a person, the FeatureOfInterest is the person and the property is its weight.

comment.en: A relation between an Observation and the entity whose quality was observed, or between an Actuation and the entity whose property was modified, or between an act of Sampling and the entity that was sampled.

example.value: The GeoSPARQL object property geo:hasLength relates (spatial) objects to datums that provide length with number and unit of measure. lis:hasQualityQuantifiedAs is suitable as a superproperty in an alignment with GeoSPARQL data that includes geo:hasLength relationships.

Links a Failure Mode to the Metric(s) used for its detection or characterization. Establishes which quantitative assessments are diagnostic indicators for specific failure types. Inverse of metricReferredBy.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of selective abstraction is not defined.

example.value: A pipeline transport activity has a "profile" part which is the variation in fluid pressure.

This relation is suitable as the top node in a taxonomy of relations of relative temporal location.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'occurs relative to' y, then x and y are 'temporal' entities, with temporal location.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of relative location in time is introduced with this relation.

example.value: A vessel is emptied before inspection, a valve is opened before draining of a pipeline, firmware is installed before the equipment begins to operate.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Resource (e.g., a Procedure or a Resource referenced by a Procedure), to the Media Type or Extent of the resource.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x is 'realized in' y, then x is a 'potential', and y is an 'activity' that successfully realizes x.

primitiveRationale.value: A full account of the notion of "successfully realizes" is beyond the scope of IDO. It depends on an account of "success", in that failed activities will not be realizations, and on the bearer of the potential participating in the realizing activity in the right way.

example.value: A pump realizes its function in a pumping (activity), a bolt realizes its function in a connection, a software monitoring application realizes its function in streaming sensor data.

Links a Countermeasure to its ordered list of steps (mqto:CountermeasureStepList). The indirection via mqto:CountermeasureStepList enables the same CountermeasureStep to be referenced in multiple Countermeasure definitions (reuse across procedures). Named hasStepList rather than hasStep to distinguish it from pko:hasStep, which links directly to step instances rather than to a list container.

comment.en: The assessment or calculation method applied by a Metric to compute its value from observations. Describes the algorithm or statistical function (e.g., 'average', 'maximum', 'standard deviation', 'trend slope') used to aggregate or analyze data within the time window.

Links a MetricAssessment to the specific ActualIshikawaItem it evaluates. In MQTO, ActualIshikawaItems are PhysicalMachine, PhysicalMaterial, and MethodExecution (actual-level counterpart of Method); domain-specific ontologies may add actual-level counterparts for Manpower and Measurement. Establishes which concrete entity the metric calculation applies to at the actual level.

definition.value: A relation between an Observation and the entity whose quality was observed, or between an Actuation and the entity whose property was modified, or between an act of Sampling and the entity that was sampled.

example.value: For example, in an Observation of the weight of a person, the FeatureOfInterest is the person and the property is its weight.

comment.en: A relation between an Observation and the entity whose quality was observed, or between an Actuation and the entity whose property was modified, or between an act of Sampling and the entity that was sampled.

A unique identifier for an entity such as a machine serial number, material batch code, signal name, or process ID. Conceptually aligned with dcterms:identifier but declared as an independent OWL DatatypeProperty (rdfs:subPropertyOf is not used because dcterms:identifier is an annotation property and OWL 2 DL does not permit a datatype property to be a subproperty of an annotation property). Enables correlation between ontology instances and external systems (ERP, MES, SCADA).

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'is about' y, then x is an 'information object' of relevance to y.

primitiveRationale.value: A comprehensive account of aboutness is beyond the scope of IDO.

scopeNote.value: This relation is suitable as the top node in a taxonomy of relations between information objects and entities, including describing, naming, characterisation, and evaluation.

example.value: A regulation describes a procedure, a datum quantifies a quality, a nameplate provides basic information about a pressure vessel, a log file traces quantity values during an activity.

comment.undefined: IAO_0000136 is 'is about', in the Information Artifact Ontology.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has feature' y, then y is a non-separable, contiguous 'arranged part' of x.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of non-separable and contiguous part is primitive.

example.value: A flange face, a corrosion protected surface, the contact surface of an electrical plug.

comment.undefined: The definition is lifted directly from 'feature whole part' of ISO 15926-2:2003.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'has role'.

comment.undefined: RO_0000081 is 'role of' in the Relations Ontology.

definition.value: Relation linking an Actuation to the Actuator that made that Actuation.

comment.en: Relation linking an Actuation to the Actuator that made that Actuation.

definition.value: Relation between an Actuation and the property of a FeatureOfInterest it is acting upon.

example.value: In the activity (Actuation) of automatically closing a window if the temperature in a room drops below 20 degrees Celsius, the property on which the Actuator acts upon is the state of the window as it changes from being open to being closed.

comment.en: Relation between an Actuation and the property of a FeatureOfInterest it is acting upon.

usageNote.value: Assume p is a pump, a an activity that realizes the function fp of p, and no subactivity x of a realizes fp (except for subactivities determined solely by restriction to a temporal sub-interval). For instance, the subactivity of the motor that drives the impeller realizes a function of the motor, but this is not by itself a realization of the function of the pump as a whole, hence the motor is not a 'primary participant'.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has primary participant' y, then x is a realization of a disposition or role of y that is essential to the identification of x.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of primary participant is not defined.

example.value: A pump is the performer in a pumping activity, a thermometer is the performer in a temperature measurement.

usageNote.value: This relation may be used where an object x is located in a site y, defined in terms of boundaries of a physical object, that may change its location over time.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'resides in' y, then x is a 'physical object', y is a 'location', and x is located entirely within y.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of being "located within" is not fully defined here, leaving open whether this is understood in terms of boundaries of physical objects or a fixed frame of reference.

example.value: An engine is located in a machine room, a pressure vessel is located in an outdoors plant area.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link an Activity (either a Procedure Execution or a Step Execution) to an Agent that had some role/responsibility in that Activity.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'quality quantified as'.

definition.value: Relation between an ActuatableProperty of a FeatureOfInterest and an Actuation changing its state.

example.value: In the activity (Actuation) of automatically closing a window if the temperature in a room drops below 20 degrees Celsius, the property on which the Actuator acts upon is the state of the window as it changes from being open to being closed.

comment.en: Relation between an ActuatableProperty of a FeatureOfInterest and an Actuation changing its state.

usageNote.value: If 'has part' relationship is modeled between Function/Disposition instances, 'has functional part' can be inferred between the objects that are bearers of the respective functions/dispositions if the corresponding rules are implemented.

'has functional part' is used to represent the functional break-down of a system. Where x is a functional part of a system y, the realisation of one or more functions of x contributes to the performance of y.

'has functional part' provides a compact way of modeling that is not expressive enough to model multiple functional breakdowns. Note that modeling 'has part' directly between Function/Disposition instances gives a higher flexibility and precision, in particular when objects have multiple functions or dispositions.
Note that, for physical objects, 'has functional part' is a specialization of 'has arranged part'.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has functional part' y, then x is a 'system' of which y is a part, and y has one or more functions that contribute to the function of x.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of "contributing function" is not fully accounted for in IDO.

example.value: A separation system has a cooling system as functional part.

usageNote.value: A literal value is uninterpreted. Provide a unit of measure, or other category, for a 'quality datum' to be interpretable.
For numeric non-integer values, the recommended data type is xsd:float.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'datum value' y, then x is a 'quality datum', and y is the literal value of x.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of literal value is primitive.

Specifies which data property of mqto:Result instances should be read when computing this Metric. A single Result instance commonly carries multiple measured values as separate data properties (e.g., ex:temperature, ex:pressure, ex:cycleTime on one PLC-cycle Result). Each Metric declares its resultProperty to select exactly one of those properties: a TemperatureMetric sets resultProperty to ex:temperature, while a PressureMetric on the same observations sets it to ex:pressure. At query time, an application reads ?metric mqto:resultProperty ?prop and then retrieves ?result ?prop ?value for each Result in the time window. Note: this is a meta-level construct — an object property whose value is itself a property — which falls outside normal OWL type-system validation. It is retained because it provides a simple, declarative way to parameterise Metrics without requiring SPARQL property-path strings or a SHACL infrastructure at the schema level.

comment.en: The result time is the instant of time when the Observation, Actuation or Sampling activity was completed.

Associates a Failure Mode with the IshikawaItem (Machine, Manpower, Material, Measurement, or Method) category it belongs to in the fishbone diagram structure. Establishes which root cause category the failure is classified under. Inverse of hasFailure.

Links a FailureOccurrence to the specific ActualIshikawaItem where it manifested. In MQTO, ActualIshikawaItems are PhysicalMachine, PhysicalMaterial, and MethodExecution (actual-level counterpart of Method); domain-specific ontologies may add actual-level counterparts for Manpower and Measurement. Establishes the concrete location or context of the failure event at the actual level.

usageNote.value: An asset model with 'implements' relationships supports compatibility checks for implementing objects, to uncover possible violations of requirements.  This is requires a transformation of the asset model, as follows: replace each 'implements'/'implementedBy' relationship with identity (owl:sameAs), and replace classifications as 'specified' or 'actual' with just 'object' for the participant individuals.  Any semantic inconsistency between the the specified object and the implementing object may then be discovered by OWL reasoning.
The mechanism for checking requirement consistency on 'implements' relationships must be implemented as a transformation that is external to the OWL ontology.

Periods of 'implements' relationships should be recorded using OWL annotations, to enable prescriptive objects to be matched with installed objects for selected points or periods in time. A detailed representation using 'role' is also possible.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'implements' y, then x is a 'specified' entity, y is an 'actual' entity, and at some time, x is the same individual as y.

explanatoryNote.value: The domain and range restrictions to disjunct classes 'actual' and 'specified' prevent chains of 'implements' relationships.

primitiveRationale.value: 'implements' is used to express identity relationships that may vary over time. Because OWL lacks time indices, the representation is informal.

example.value: Pump P-101 is a tag, and ACME pumps with serial numbers 457636 and 457711 implement (have been installed as) this pump.

scopeNote.value: The 'implements' relation is central to the intended use of IDO. It allows for a record of different individuals being installed or otherwise serving as a specified object over time. Typically, a serial-numbered physical artefact is installed as a "specification" individual (a "tag") at one time, and later replaced with another serial-numbered physical artefact. The 'implements' relation carries semantic restrictions only to ensure no object is both Actual and Specified. No further constraints are given, to allow 'implements' relationships even where restrictions on the specification object are inconsistent with those on the implementing (installed) object.

comment.en: Links a FailureOccurrence to the MetricAssessment(s) that detected or triggered it. Establishes which metric evaluations identified the failure through threshold violations. Inverse of raised.

Links a Failure Mode to the Metric(s) used for its detection or characterization. Establishes which quantitative assessments are diagnostic indicators for specific failure types. Inverse of metricReferredBy.

Links a Failure Mode to another Failure Mode that causes it, forming a causal chain in root cause analysis. This general property has three sub-properties (causedByWeakly, causedByModerately, causedByStrongly) to express different causal strengths. Inverse of causes.

Links a Failure Mode to another Failure Mode that it causes, forming a causal chain in root cause analysis. This general property has three sub-properties (causesWeakly, causesModerately, causesStrongly) to express different causal strengths. Inverse of causedBy.

Associates a Failure Mode with the IshikawaItem (Machine, Manpower, Material, Measurement, or Method) category it belongs to in the fishbone diagram structure. Establishes which root cause category the failure is classified under. Inverse of hasFailure.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Role In Time, that is the role that an Agent holds for a certain Period of Time, to the Role that is held.

Links a Countermeasure to its ordered list of steps (mqto:CountermeasureStepList). The indirection via mqto:CountermeasureStepList enables the same CountermeasureStep to be referenced in multiple Countermeasure definitions (reuse across procedures). Named hasStepList rather than hasStep to distinguish it from pko:hasStep, which links directly to step instances rather than to a list container.

comment.en: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to represent the cause of an issue, encountered during a Procedure/Step Execution, as identified and defined by the user.

Links a Countermeasure to the Failure Mode(s) it can remediate or reduce (specified level). Establishes which failure types a remediation procedure addresses. Inverse of mitigatedBy. The actual-level counterparts are mqto:mitigatedOccurrence and mqto:mitigatedByExecution.

Links a CountermeasureExecution to the FailureOccurrence it addressed (actual level). Inverse of mitigatedByExecution. The specified-level counterpart is mqto:mitigates (domain: Countermeasure, range: FailureMode).

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has assembled part' y, then y is a connected 'arranged part' of x.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of connection is primitive.

example.value: A pipe bend welded into a piping spool, the protective sheath on an electrical cable, a door-handle in a door, a storey of a building.

definition.value: Relation linking an Observation to the property that was observed. The ObservableProperty should be a property of the FeatureOfInterest (linked by hasFeatureOfInterest) of this Observation.

comment.en: Relation linking an Observation to the property that was observed. The ObservableProperty should be a property of the FeatureOfInterest (linked by hasFeatureOfInterest) of this Observation.

usageNote.value: If 'has part' relationship is modeled between Function/Disposition instances, 'has functional part' can be inferred between the objects that are bearers of the respective functions/dispositions if the corresponding rules are implemented.

'has functional part' is used to represent the functional break-down of a system. Where x is a functional part of a system y, the realisation of one or more functions of x contributes to the performance of y.

'has functional part' provides a compact way of modeling that is not expressive enough to model multiple functional breakdowns. Note that modeling 'has part' directly between Function/Disposition instances gives a higher flexibility and precision, in particular when objects have multiple functions or dispositions.
Note that, for physical objects, 'has functional part' is a specialization of 'has arranged part'.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has functional part' y, then x is a 'system' of which y is a part, and y has one or more functions that contribute to the function of x.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of "contributing function" is not fully accounted for in IDO.

example.value: A separation system has a cooling system as functional part.

usageNote.value: With OWL property chains, the right kind of participation can be secured for activities that count as realizations of 'potential' individuals. As in, a bolt is a passive participant in a bolt-driving activity, where its disposition to enter a threaded hole is realized (distinct from the bolt's function, which is realized in a subsequent activity of holding pieces together).

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: if x 'has passive participant' y, then x is a passive participant in y.

primitiveRationale.value: The distinction between active and participation participation is primitive.

example.value: A metal plate is perforated in a drilling activity, a volume of liquid is heated in a heat transmission activity, a loading ramp is the location of an offloading activity, a fluid medium is pumped, a piece of metal is shaped, a stream of data is filtered.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'datumUOM'.

comment.undefined: Relation from 'unit of measure' to 'quantity datum'. The inverse relation of datumUOM.

comment.en: Indicates that an Observation or Actuation serves as the data foundation for a MetricAssessment calculation. Inverse of basedOn. Enables tracing from raw sensor data or actuation events to computed metrics.

A numeric score or confidence value assigned to a CountermeasureExecution recommendation. Used by diagnostic systems to rank countermeasure suggestions based on relevance, likelihood of success, or other scoring criteria. The interpretation and scale of scores is application-specific.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has quality' y, then x is an 'object', and y is a 'quality' that is dependent on x.

primitiveRationale.value: The relation of dependence is not defined in IDO.

example.value: A motor has rated output power, a lightbulb has luminance, an amount of liquid has mass, a bar of metal has chemical composition, a document has readability.

comment.undefined: RO_0000086 is 'has quality' in the Relations Ontology.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of maintaining connection is not defined; what counts as a direct connection is hard to define precisely.

example.value: An electrical plug is connected to a socket, a tag plate is welded to a pressure vessel.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: x 'has capability' y if and only if x 'has potential' y, and y is a 'capability'.

example.value: A pressure sensor has the capability of being a level sensor, a wall has the capability of noise insulation.

If the minRepetitions is equal to 1, it means that the Step is mandatory. If the minRepetitions is equal to 0, it means that the Step is optional.

example.value: The moment an electrical connection is broken, the time that a fluid reaches a set temperature.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has sublocation' y, then x and y are locations, and x 'has part' y.

example.value: The landing area is a sublocation of the helicopter deck.

comment.en: The number of production items (shots, batches, or cycles) over which a Metric aggregates observations for calculation. Complements timeWindowSize for item-count-based windows, e.g., 'last N shots'. Either or both window parameters may be set depending on whether the metric uses a time-based, item-based, or combined aggregation window.
The timestamp when a CountermeasureStepExecution was successfully completed. Marks the end of a remediation step's execution. If a step is aborted, abortedTime should be used instead.

Associates a Signal (data channel) with the IshikawaItem (Machine, Manpower, Material, Measurement, or Method) that provides it. Establishes the source of measurement or control data channels. Inverse of providesSignal.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Procedure Execution or Step Execution to a User Question Occurrence, that is the occurrence of a question that the user came up with during execution.

comment.en: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Procedure Execution or Step Execution to a User Question Occurrence, that is the occurrence of a question that the user came up with during execution.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'site of' y, then x is a 'site', y is an 'activity', and y takes place entirely within x.

primitiveRationale.value: x 'site of' y means, the spatial extent of the site contains the spatial extent of the activity for the duration of the activity. This requires a relation of "spatial projection throughout the time of the activity", which is not included in IDO. (The BFO-ISO definition expresses this using the temporalized relation 'spatially projects onto at all times'.)

example.value: Mixing takes place within a vessel, transport takes place in a pipeline

comment.undefined: BFO_0000067 is 'contains process', from RO, where an annotation says the 'editor preferred term' is "site of". There are subtle differences in the range and domain restrictions between BFO's 'environs' and RO's 'contains process': where RO has domain as the broad 'independent continuant' only, BFO has ('site' or 'material entity'); where RO has range as 'occurrent', BFO has ('process' or 'process boundary').

comment.en: Indicates a strong causal relationship where one Failure Mode is caused by another with high probability or significant influence. Enables ordinal ranking of causal strengths without requiring precise numeric probabilities. Subproperty of causedBy.

The minimum acceptable threshold value for a Metric, as a dimensionless double. MetricAssessments compare their calculatedValue against this bound; values below the lowerBound indicate a threshold violation. The unit of measurement is determined by the Metric's associated Signal.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'implements'

comment.en: A relation from a Sensor to the Stimulus that the Sensor detects. The Stimulus itself will be serving as a proxy for some ObservableProperty.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'has interest'.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has activity part' y, then x and y are activities, and x 'has arranged part' y.

scopeNote.value: Parts of activities may be determined by a range of criteria, including temporal restrictions (to sub-periods), spatial restrictions (parts that occupy sub-volumes of space), by the engagement of participants (temporal or spatial parts where one or more participants are active), and "selective abstraction" to profiles (parts determined by that which affects variation in selected qualities).

example.value: The startup period of a chemical process, the inspection part of a maintenance activity, the moment a software program terminates execution.

comment.undefined: BFO_0000117 is 'has occurrent part' in BFO-ISO. This relation has 'occurrent' as domain and range, where the IDO 'has activity part' is restricted to just 'activity'.

usageNote.value: A literal value is uninterpreted. Provide a unit of measure, or other category, for a 'quality datum' to be interpretable.
For numeric non-integer values, the recommended data type is xsd:float.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'datum value' y, then x is a 'quality datum', and y is the literal value of x.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of literal value is primitive.

example.value: The measured datum "55 degrees Celsius" has "55" as literal value.

comment.undefined: hasSimpleResult is a data property in the Semantic Sensor Networks ontology.

usageNote.value: The parts do not have to be connected to each other.  They may be separable or non-separable.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has arranged part' y, then y is a part of x with a boundary determined in relation to other parts of x.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of determinate boundary is taken as primitive. For further details, refer to the explanation  of "fiat part" in the BFO documentation.

example.value: The upper half of a pressure vessel, the mid-section of a bridge. The buildings on a campus, arranged as parts.

comment.undefined: BFO_0000024 is 'fiat object part' in BFO-ISO.

usageNote.value: The relationship x 'has part' y implies that y  is physically or conceptually associated with x, contributing to its  structure, functionality, or characteristics.
Where possible, the sub-properties should be used. This property can be used for arbitrary aggregates.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has part' y, then y is a part of x.

primitiveRationale.value: Parthood is a primitive for ontologies.

example.value: A system has functional parts, a process has stages, a plant is partitioned into areas.

comment.undefined: BFO_0000117 is 'has occurrent part' in BFO-ISO.

definition.value: Relation between an ObservableProperty and the Sensor able to observe it.

comment.en: Relation between an ObservableProperty and the Sensor able to observe it.

definition.value: Relation between a FeatureOfInterest and the Sample used to represent it.

comment.en: Relation between a FeatureOfInterest and the Sample used to represent it.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'site of'.

comment.undefined: BFO_0000066 is 'occurs in'

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has content part' y, then x and y are information objects, and x 'has part' y.

example.value: A part of a document such as a chapter, section or figure

comment.undefined: Introduced February 2024 as superproperty for parts of information objects.

comment.en: Associates an IshikawaItem (Machine, Manpower, Material, Measurement, or Method) with a Failure Mode that can occur in that category. Establishes which failures belong to each root cause category in the fishbone diagram structure. Inverse of failureBelongsTo.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'has quality quantified as'.
For annotations, see the inverse relation 'has activity part'.
For annotations, see the inverse relation 'before'.

comment.undefined: BFO_0000062 is 'preceded by' in BFO-ISO.

definition.value: A relation from a Stimulus to the Property that the Stimulus is serving as a proxy for.

example.value: For example, the expansion of quicksilver is a stimulus that serves as a proxy for some temperature property. An increase or decrease in the velocity of spinning cups on a wind sensor is serving as a proxy for the wind speed.

comment.en: A relation from a Stimulus to the Property that the Stimulus is serving as a proxy for.

Links a FailureOccurrence to the specific ActualIshikawaItem where it manifested. In MQTO, ActualIshikawaItems are PhysicalMachine, PhysicalMaterial, and MethodExecution (actual-level counterpart of Method); domain-specific ontologies may add actual-level counterparts for Manpower and Measurement. Establishes the concrete location or context of the failure event at the actual level.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Step Execution to its direct previous Step Execution.

comment.en: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Step Execution to its direct previous Step Execution.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has temporal part' y, then x and y are temporal entities, and y is a part of x.

example.value: A specific day is a temporal part of a specific week.

definition.value: Relation between a Sensor and an ObservableProperty that it is capable of sensing.

comment.en: Relation between a Sensor and an ObservableProperty that it is capable of sensing.

Relates a MetricAssessment to the Property (ObservableProperty or ActuableProperty) it evaluated (actual level). Counterpart to mqto:readsFrom, which relates a Metric to a Signal in the specified level.

Links a MetricAssessment to the Observation(s) or Actuation(s) used as input data for its calculation. Establishes the data provenance chain from raw sensor readings or actuation events to computed metrics. Inverse of baseFor.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to represent whether a Procedure is a Template or not. A Procedure is a Template if it is associated with a preset of Steps that can be used to define an Executable Procedure. Such steps are not directly executable in the form they are specified.

comment.en: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to represent whether a Procedure is a Template or not. A Procedure is a Template if it is associated with a preset of Steps that can be used to define an Executable Procedure. Such steps are not directly executable in the form they are specified.

usageNote.value: The range of 'quality quantified as' is 'quality datum', which includes data that do not follow the "unit of measure with value" pattern. Examples are colours and chemical composition categories.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x is a 'quality quantified as' y, then x is an 'quality' that is 'represented in' the 'quality datum' y.

example.value: The nominal outer diameter of a pipe is 324 mm, the measured temperature of a boiler is 150 degrees Celsius, the colour of a surface is 'red'.

comment.undefined: IAO_0000417 is 'is quality measured as' of the Information Artifact Ontology. In IDO, the term "quantified" replaces "measured" to support cases where measurement is not involved, as in, e.g., estimates.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'contains'.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Step to the Expertise Level, meaning that the Step, or MultiStep, is targeted at persons with a certain level of expertise (e.g., junior, senior).

comment.en: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Step to the Expertise Level, meaning that the Step, or MultiStep, is targeted at persons with a certain level of expertise (e.g., junior, senior).

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'quality quantified as'.

comment.en: The maximum acceptable threshold value for a Metric, as a dimensionless double. MetricAssessments compare their calculatedValue against this bound; values above the upperBound indicate a threshold violation. The unit of measurement is determined by the Metric's associated Signal.

usageNote.value: This relation is "abstract" (in the object-orientation sense of the word) in that it serves only as a parent relation to specialized relations of relative location.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x is 'located relative to' y, then x and y are 'object's with location in space.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of relative location in space is introduced with this relation.

example.value: An outlet is below an inlet, a door is inside an opening, a motor is inside an enclosure.

comment.undefined: BFO_0000210 is 'occupies spatial region at some time' in BFO-ISO. This relation has domain restricted to 'independent continuant' and not 'spatial region', and range to 'spatial region', meaning it's a relation from entities that are material objects or determined by material objects, to spatial regions determined independently of material objects.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Role In Time, that is the role that an Agent holds for a certain Period of Time, to the Resource (e.g., a Procedure) that the Role relates to.

comment.en: The actual computed numeric value of the metric assessment. This preserves the quantitative result before threshold comparison, enabling trend analysis, statistical modeling, and root cause correlation. For example, if a temperature metric calculates 85.7°C and the upperBound is 80°C, calculatedValue is 85.7 while assessmentValue would be true (indicating a violation). The unit of measurement is inherited from the implemented Metric specification.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: if x 'creates' y, then x is an 'activity', y is an 'object', and x brings y into being.

primitiveRationale.value: A comprehensive definition of the notion of creation is beyond the scope of IDO.

example.value: A manufacturing activity produces a car, a welding activity produces a piping spool, a measurement activity produces a performance log.

comment.undefined: ISO 15926-2:2003, 5.2.10.2 provides an example as, "A car manufacturing activity causes the beginning of a car".

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a version of a Procedure to its direct previous version of the same Procedure.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: x 'has role' y if and only if x 'has potential' y, and y is a 'role'.

example.value: An window has the role of a ventilation opening, a room has the role of a storage space, a gas sensor has the role of a leak detector, a person has the role of inspector.

comment.undefined: Inspired by BFO's "has role" (RO_0000087)

usageNote.value: A property chain axiom secures that when an activity has a 'profile quantity datum', the bearer of the quantity is a participant in the activity.
A property chain axiom secures that when a realizable (a 'disposition' or 'role') is 'realized in' an activity, the activity has the bearer as a participant.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has participant' y, then x is an 'activity', and y is an 'object' that participates in x.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of participation is primitive.

example.value: A pump participates in (a) pumping (activity), an electric transformer participates in voltage conversion, a stream of water participates in dilution, a bolt participates in a connection.

comment.undefined: BFO_0000057 is 'has participant at some time'.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x is 'before' y, then x ends no later than the start of y.

primitiveRationale.value: The temporal vocabulary is primitive.

example.value: The cooling stage takes place before separation.

comment.undefined: BFO_0000063 is 'precedes' in BFO-ISO.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'has activity profile'.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to represent the feedback on the procedure that was provided by an Agent during the Procedure or Step Execution.

comment.en: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to represent the feedback on the procedure that was provided by an Agent during the Procedure or Step Execution.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has ending' y, then y is an 'event' which 'has temporal extent' some 'instant' which is the last instant of the temporal extent of x.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of last instant is not defined.

example.value: The event that light is switched off, the end of filling a tank.

comment.undefined: BFO_0000224 is 'has last instant' in BFO-ISO, defined for 'temporal region' (differently from 'has ending' in IDO, defined for 'activity').

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'has capability'.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to represent the changelog description of a Procedure, that details all updates with respect to the previous version of the same Procedure.

comment.en: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to represent the changelog description of a Procedure, that details all updates with respect to the previous version of the same Procedure.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Step Execution to an Entity that was generated during execution, and that may correspond to an output Variable of the Step.
In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Procedure, for which we want to manage version control, to its current version.
In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to represent the end of a Period Of Time.

In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to represent a URL of a Resource (e.g., a Procedure or a document referenced by a Procedure) that gives access to a distribution of it.

comment.en: Links a FailureOccurrence to a CountermeasureExecution that was recommended in response to it (actual level). Captures the decision to apply a countermeasure following a detected failure. Inverse of suggestedBy. Distinct from mqto:mitigatedOccurrence, which records that a countermeasure execution actually resolved the failure.

usageNote.value: A property chain axiom ensures that a quantity datum individual associated with an activity profile by 'profile quantity datum' quantifies a quality which defines the activity profile by 'profile of quality'.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: x is a 'profile of quality' y if and only if x is an 'activity profile' that varies only in the 'quality' y.

example.value: A temperature activity profile of a heating process represents just that part of the process which is a variation in temperature.

scopeNote.value: 'profile of quality' should be considered a functional property (i.e., an 'activity profile' is a profile of only a single 'quality'). However, in OWL, an object property with property chain restrictions may not be combined with cardinality restrictions. Correct cardinalities must therefore be maintained with rules external to the ontology.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has quality' y, then x is an 'object', and y is a 'quality' that is dependent on x.

primitiveRationale.value: The relation of dependence is not defined in IDO.

example.value: A motor has rated output power, a lightbulb has luminance, an amount of liquid has mass, a bar of metal has chemical composition, a document has readability.

comment.undefined: RO_0000086 is 'has quality' in the Relations Ontology.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'has disposition'.

comment.en: The duration of the time window over which a Metric aggregates observations for calculation, expressed as an xsd:duration value (e.g., "PT5M"^^xsd:duration for 5 minutes, "PT1H"^^xsd:duration for 1 hour). Defines how far back in time from the calculatedTime the metric should consider observation data.

usageNote.value: This relation is intended to be used where an object x is located inside another object y, but x not is not a part of y (as would be the case, e.g., for an assembly).

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'contains' y, then x and y are 'physical object's, and y is located entirely within x.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of being "located within" is not fully defined here, leaving open whether this is understood in terms of boundaries of physical objects or a fixed frame of reference.

example.value: An instrument is contained in an enclosure, an pump contains an impeller, a device is contained by a volume of inert gas, a pipeline inspection gauge (pig) is located inside a pipeline.

comment.undefined: RO_0001015 is 'location of' in the Relations Ontology.

usageNote.value: A property chain axiom secures that when an activity has a 'profile quantity datum', the bearer of the quantity is a participant in the activity.
A property chain axiom secures that when a realizable (a 'disposition' or 'role') is 'realized in' an activity, the activity has the bearer as a participant.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has participant' y, then x is an 'activity', and y is an 'object' that participates in x.

primitiveRationale.value: The notion of participation is primitive.

example.value: A pump participates in (a) pumping (activity), an electric transformer participates in voltage conversion, a stream of water participates in dilution, a bolt participates in a connection.

comment.undefined: BFO_0000057 is 'has participant at some time'.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'has passive participant'.
For annotations, see the inverse relation 'has potential'.

comment.undefined: This relation is used in the property chain for 'has participant', for inferring participation in a temporal entity (typically, an activity).

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a User Question Occurrence to a Resource that addresses/answers to the question that the user came up with during the Procedure/Step Execution.

comment.en: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a User Question Occurrence to a Resource that addresses/answers to the question that the user came up with during the Procedure/Step Execution.

usageNote.value: For annotations, see the inverse relation 'has function'.

An RDL can include parthood relations that identify parts by their materials, supporting recyclability efforts and similar use cases.  This relation is suitable as the top node of a taxonomy of such relations. These include "reinforced with material"  or "has a matrix with material" which cannot be aligned with  lis:hasAssembledPart or lis:hasArrangedPart.

naturalLanguageDefinition.value: If x 'has material part' y, then x is a 'physical object', and y is a maximal part of x that has a certain 'material composition quality'.

primitiveRationale.value: The reference in the definition to some 'material composition quality' possessed by the part can not be captured in OWL.

example.value: A laptop cover has aluminium and plastic material parts.

scopeNote.value: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Step to its direct next Step.

comment.en: In the context of the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO), this property is used to link a Step to its direct next Step.

Relates an IshikawaItem (Machine, Manpower, Material, Measurement, or Method) to the data channels (Signals) associated with it. A Signal may be an output channel carrying measurement data from the item (e.g. a temperature sensor reading) or an input channel carrying control data to the item (e.g. a setpoint or actuator command). The Signal is a specified channel descriptor; actual observations are made via sosa:Observation on the corresponding ActualIshikawaItem.

definition.value: The simple value of an Observation or Actuation or act of Sampling.

example.value: For instance, the values 23 or true.

comment.en: The simple value of an Observation or Actuation or act of Sampling.
